description:
## Policy change

Today (ir_typer.rs:238–242): *"the any-key only exists when the fn is
closure-reachable, entry-seeded, or genuinely needs the opaque-dispatch
fallback. Direct-call-only fns have no any-key."*

Flip the policy: **every reachable FnId gets an any-key spec.** Symmetric for
reachable conts: every cont fn gets an any-key entry in the same registry
(slot 0 = `Descr::any()`, captures = `Descr::any()`).

This is the floor. Narrowed specs continue to land above it as pure
optimization.

## Implementation sketch

In `type_module`, after the existing reachability-driven spec generation, walk
`module.fns` and:
- for each `f` with at least one registered spec (i.e. reachable), if the
  any-key (`vec![Descr::any(); arity]`) is not present, type `f` once more
  with that key and register.
- same for conts (`module.fns` includes conts; check by `kind` or by
  `is_cont_fn`).

`any_key_specs` index becomes total over reachable FnIds — currently partial.

## Tests

- Spec-table golden test: every reachable FnId in a fixture has an any-key entry.
- Unit test: a fn called only with `int` still gets an any-key spec registered.
- Existing `expected.specs` goldens get blessed with the new any-key entries.
- Spec count must not regress wildly: assert `n_specs_after <= 1.5 * n_specs_before` on `ast_eval` and `list_primitives` fixtures (sanity).

## Independent of (1) and (2). Blocks (4).

comments.text:
fz-aiz.3 revised — see also session findings on the data-model conflation.

Original plan was naive: adding any-keys to module_types.specs is not a
passive enrichment, it is a codegen instruction. Three roles are bundled
under that one map's any-key membership:
  1. type inference under widest inputs
  2. codegen emits a Tagged-ABI body at SpecId == FnId.0
  3. closure routing: MakeClosure's body_func_id picks via
     spec_registry.resolve(fid, [any; n] + captures), and exact-matches
     the any-key when present

Roles 2 and 3 are gated by typer reachability (fz-vw4 / fz-pky.2).
Adding an any-key for a direct-only fn flips role 3 from "fall back via
iter to a narrow spec" to "exact-match the any-key" — making
MakeClosure store the Tagged-ABI body in the closure value while
narrow callers expect RawInt ABI. ABI mismatch on `apply2(double, 21)`
produced `5 = 21 >> 2`.

Reframe — small fix, not the intrinsic/adapter arc:

1. ensure_any_keys: register an any-key spec for every reachable FnId
   (the soundness floor for resolve fallback).
2. MakeClosure body_func_id selection (ir_codegen.rs:6300): stop using
   resolve([any; n] + captures); add `closure_canonical_body(fid,
   captures)` that consults the typer's opaque-dispatch reachability
   flag — if opaque dispatch is reachable, pick any-key (Tagged ABI);
   otherwise pick the deterministic canonical narrow spec. Decouples
   role 3 from role 1.
3. Narrow call sites that today exact-match a narrow spec are unaffected
   (their query Descrs are concrete, not all-any).

The intrinsic-and-adapter design is unnecessary: the any-key body itself
serves as the dispatch fallback target. resolve()'s subsumption already
prefers narrow when one covers; any-key is naturally picked only when
no narrow covers. aiz.4 wires the actual fallback edge; aiz.5 replaces
the panics with that fallback at call sites.

title: fz-aiz.3: mandatory any-key spec for every reachable FnId + Cont
